item.title: UK household energy bills to rise by 13% from July to two-year high
item.description: Ofgem’s cap increase means annual energy costs will rise to £1,862

item.title: Byju’s founder sentenced to jail in Singapore for contempt of court
item.description: Judge orders six-month jail term for edtech group’s chief Byju Raveendran

item.title: AI’s mixed economic signals
item.description: Plus, the euro convergence trade

item.title: Trad bookies ♥ prediction markets
item.description: C-c-c-c-combo rakers

item.title: Samsung workers set for $400,000 bonus after deal to share AI profits
item.description: Agreement with union ends wrangling over how to share spoils of boom at memory-chip maker

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: Halifax and TSB have outlived their brand-name usefulness
item.description: Bank consolidation in Britain now makes sense in targeting customers for multiple products

item.title: Bullying problem? The battle inside BP’s boardroom
item.description: With the ousting of Albert Manifold, BP has replaced two chief executives and two chairs in the past three years

item.title: UK retailers call for £2.60 small parcels charge to close customs loophole
item.description: M&S, Primark and Next want government to close exemption benefiting Chinese rivals

item.title: France signals volte-face on ‘Made in Europe’ subsidies for UK car industry
item.description: Paris initially lukewarm on including Britain in European Commission proposal to shield strategic industries from imports

item.title: UK software investor Hg strikes first buyout deal since SaaSpocalypse
item.description: Acquisition of rights and royalties management company for $500mn comes after plunge in software stocks

item.title: Hong Kong overtakes Switzerland as hub for global offshore wealth
item.description: Chinese territory enjoys surge of investment from mainland as wealthy spread assets across different jurisdictions

item.title: Netflix hits out over German plan to make groups invest more in Europe
item.description: Streamer among those criticising proposal as protectionist as government looks to boost industry

item.title: The pizza oven start-up that came through the fire
item.description: Co-founders of Scotland’s Ooni faced a post-pandemic crisis after invention went global

item.title: Can GLP-1 drugs save the mall?
item.description: The advent of diet-driven shopping comes at a crucial time for many retailers

item.title: ExxonMobil Texas move ignites battle over shareholder rights
item.description: Investors will decide on Wednesday whether to approve oil major’s reincorporation as it seeks to parry investor activism

item.title: EU defence chief urges states to stop making ‘haute couture’ missiles
item.description: Andrius Kubilius pushes for governments to open weapons stockpiles to Ukraine

item.title: KPMG hunts Silicon Valley AI disrupters to Big Four model
item.description: Firm looks to partner or invest in start-ups that could otherwise threaten its business model

item.title: For failing water utilities, nationalisation is not a dirty word
item.description: It is easier to fund maintenance and infrastructure upgrades when freed from the temptation to pay big dividends

item.title: China overhauls world’s biggest surveillance network with advanced AI
item.description: Local police forces are modernising the country’s ageing infrastructure with more powerful tracking systems

item.title: How AI threatens the giants of consulting
item.description: The technology opens the door for smaller, well-funded challengers to take market share from the Big Four and others

item.title: BP shakes up its leadership … again
item.description: The oil major removed chair Albert Manifold over ‘serious concerns’ about his conduct

item.title: The 18th-century woman who made saving possible for the poor
item.description: She set up England’s first 'penny bank', an early exercise in financial inclusion

item.title: AI Labs: Elon Musk wants AI in space
item.description: Will SpaceX’s giant IPO turbocharge Elon Musk’s AI ambitions?

item.title: The world’s biggest EV maker weans itself off supply-chain finance
item.description: Borrowings soar as BYD bows to Beijing’s pressure on treatment of component suppliers

item.title: Bezos’s Blue Origin secures $468mn in Nasa Moon Base contracts
item.description: SpaceX absent from first round of commercial agreements for US space agency’s $20bn project

item.title: BP chair’s departure turns spotlight on Meg O’Neill to deliver swift turnaround
item.description: Albert Manifold’s behaviour and use of personal devices cited as factors in his removal over conduct and governance concerns

item.title: BP removes chair Albert Manifold after claims of bullying
item.description: Hands-on approach was viewed as aggressive by several colleagues at UK oil major

item.title: FirstFT: ByteDance offers AI team special stock to combat poaching
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: BP removes chair Albert Manifold, and Iran accuses US of ‘flagrant’ ceasefire violations

item.title: SpaceX nears lift-off
item.description: An IPO so big it could change the Nasdaq
